---
title: "Murmurations"
subtitle: "A Quarto + Reveal.js slide template with a simulated background flock"
author: "Michel Nivard"
format: revealjs
---

## What This Project Is

This repository is a working Quarto presentation that uses the `revealjs` format and adds a custom animated background.

- Quarto provides the authoring workflow
- Reveal.js provides the HTML slide engine
- `theme.scss` sets the visual style
- `flock.js` runs the murmuration simulation in the background

---

## Why Quarto And Reveal.js

Quarto lets you write slides in Markdown and render them to a Reveal.js presentation.

- Slides are created with Markdown headings
- Quarto renders those headings into Reveal.js sections
- Reveal.js handles navigation, transitions, speaker view, and browser presentation
- The official Quarto Reveal.js guide is at <https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/>

For beginners, the main thing to know is that you edit `index.qmd`, run `quarto render`, and Quarto generates a complete HTML slide deck for you.

---

## Visual Design

The background is intentionally atmospheric rather than literal.

- A dawn-colored gradient sets the sky
- Three faint low-poly hill bands give the scene depth
- The flock is rendered as many small dark birds with perspective-based sizing
- Birds prefer the right side so they interfere with slide text less often

---

## How The Simulation Works

The flock is not just random motion. It is a lightweight 3D boid model tuned to feel like a murmuration.

At each animation frame:

1. Each bird looks at nearby birds in a local spatial grid.
2. It reacts to a fixed number of nearest neighbors rather than every bird on screen.
3. Neighbors to the side count more strongly than neighbors behind, which makes the motion feel more like a real flock.
4. The bird updates `x`, `y`, and `z`, so depth is part of the flock logic rather than only a size trick.
5. Depth is projected back to the screen so birds that are closer look larger and shift position slightly.

---

## What Happens On Slide Change

Slide changes trigger a disturbance wave rather than a global explosion.

- A local wave is injected into the flock
- Birds near the wave turn first
- The turning impulse propagates through the flock over time
- This makes transitions feel more like a startle spreading through a group

---

## The Main Tuning File

Most behavior lives in `flock.js`.

```js
const CONFIG = {
  boidCount: 750,
  topologicalNeighbors: 9,
  separationRadius: 18,
  rightAffinityStrength: 0.11,
  waveSpeed: 13,
  waveWidth: 95
};
```

If you want to change the feel of the simulation, this is the first file to edit.

---

## What To Tweak

The easiest controls for experimentation are:

- `boidCount`: total number of birds
- `baseSpeed`, `minSpeed`, `maxSpeed`: how quickly the flock moves
- `separationRadius`: how tightly birds pack together
- `rightAffinityStrength`: how strongly the flock prefers the right side
- `waveSpeed`, `waveWidth`, `waveStrength`: how slide-change disturbances propagate
- `minDrawSize`, `maxDrawSize`: how strongly depth affects bird size

Use `theme.scss` when you want to change color, typography, spacing, or the overall atmosphere rather than the flock mechanics.

---

## Use This Template

To make your own deck:

1. Edit `index.qmd`
2. Run `quarto preview` while writing
3. Run `quarto render` when you want the final HTML output
4. Commit the rendered `docs/` folder if you want to publish with GitHub Pages

This demo is designed to be copied and modified rather than treated as a closed theme.
